title: ingress.ready
description: Published by a receiver after a chunk has landed in bronze and GCS. Consumed by the streaming consumer to begin ELT processing.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Incremented only on breaking changes.

trace_id.description: Unique identifier for this ingress event. Propagates through every stage.

tenant_id.description: Internal tenant UUID, identical to Customer Master core.tenants.id and identity_mirror.tenants.tenant_id. Load-bearing identity.

store_id.description: Internal store UUID, identical to Customer Master core.stores.id and identity_mirror.stores.store_id. Load-bearing identity.

tenant_display_code.description: Customer Master's authoritative external code for the tenant (tenants.display_code, e.g. 'buc-ees'). Optional in the schema, but producers MUST populate it when publishing. Readability only, never a substitute for tenant_id.

store_code.description: Customer Master's authoritative external code for the store (stores.store_code). Optional in the schema, but producers MUST populate it when present in Customer Master (the source column is nullable). Readability only, never a substitute for store_id.

source_id.description: Source registration identifier (POS type, ERP type, etc.).

template_id.description: The mapping template (config.source_mappings template lineage, D68) this payload was uploaded against. Passed through verbatim from csv.received by the worker and persisted to bronze for replay lineage. NOT consumed for mapping selection until Slice 8a (D71: the streaming consumer parses but ignores it; hard gate, no second ACTIVE template per source before 8a).

bronze_ref.description: Foreign key into the bronze metadata table.

gcs_uri.description: Full GCS URI of the raw payload. Path scheme enforced by libs/dis-storage; the tenant segment is the internal tenant UUID (not an external code), because the UUID is immutable while external codes are user-editable.

received_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the receiver accepted the request.

replay.description: True if this is a replay (Ithina-side or tenant-side). False or absent for fresh ingress.

parent_trace_id.description: If replay=true, the trace_id of the original event. Optional.
